summary: Feature request to move Linux native builds away from Cake to a bash script or Makefile, removing the Mono dependency for Linux build contributors.

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Linux native build pipeline, Cake/Mono dependency

analysis.summary: The reporter (a project maintainer) requests that Linux native builds be moved out of Cake into a simpler bash script or Makefile, citing Mono as an unnecessary extra requirement for Linux build contributors. As of triage, the native/linux/build.cake file still exists and Cake is still used for Linux builds via build.sh invoking dotnet cake.
analysis.rationale: The issue title uses [FEATURE] and the body describes a new capability (replacing Cake with a bash/Makefile alternative for Linux). This is a feature request to change the build tooling, not a bug in SkiaSharp functionality. The area is Build since it concerns the build infrastructure. The feature has not yet been implemented — native/linux/build.cake still exists and build.sh still calls dotnet cake.

codeInvestigation.finding: Linux native build is defined in a Cake script — the Cake dependency for Linux builds is confirmed still present.
The top-level build.sh still calls `dotnet cake $@`, meaning all targets including Linux go through Cake. No bash/Makefile alternative exists for Linux.

keySignals.text: Building SkiaSharp does not actually use it, and we can maybe have a bash script or Makefile to do this.
keySignals.interpretation: Explicit feature request to replace Cake with simpler tooling for Linux builds.

keySignals.text: Cake requires mono, and although it is pretty cross-platform, it is an additional requirement.
keySignals.interpretation: The motivation is removing the Mono/Cake dependency from the Linux build path.

resolution.hypothesis: Replace native/linux/build.cake with a bash script or Makefile that invokes GN/ninja directly to build libSkiaSharp and libHarfBuzzSharp for Linux, allowing CI to build without requiring dotnet/Cake on Linux builders.

proposals.title: Add bash build script for Linux
proposals.description: Create a build.sh in native/linux/ that directly invokes GN configuration and ninja build, mirroring what build.cake does but without requiring Mono or Cake. The main build.sh can delegate to this for Linux targets.

proposals.title: Keep Cake but document Mono requirement clearly
proposals.description: As a lower-effort alternative, document clearly that Linux builds require dotnet (not mono) since Cake now runs on dotnet. This may already be addressed if the project moved to dotnet-based Cake.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add bash build script for Linux
resolution.recommendedReason: Directly addresses the stated goal of removing the Cake/Mono dependency from Linux builds.

actionability.reason: Valid build infrastructure feature request filed by a maintainer. The Linux build still uses Cake as of triage. No blocking urgency but worth pursuing to simplify CI contributor setup.

actions.description: Apply feature-request, Build, and Linux labels
